prerequisites: A Dixa account with administrator privileges. Only administrators can generate API tokens; agents do not have the required permission.
A Dixa API token to set as the required `api_token` field.
A `start_date` value marking the earliest point from which to pull records (the connector pulls records with `updated_at >= start_date`).

authMethods.name: API token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a Dixa API token supplied via the `api_token` field. Dixa expects the token in the HTTP `Authorization` header using the Bearer scheme (`Authorization: Bearer <token>`). Generate the token in Dixa as an administrator under Settings > Integrations > API Tokens, selecting the "Dixa API" version.

setupSteps.title: Generate a Dixa API token
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Dixa as an administrator. Go to Settings, open the Integrations page, and select the API Tokens tab. Click "Add API token", give it a descriptive name, select "Dixa API" as the API version, and click "Save changes". Copy the generated token immediately for use in the connector.

setupSteps.title: Set the API token
setupSteps.body: Paste the copied token into the required `api_token` field. The connector sends it to Dixa as a Bearer token in the Authorization header to authorize requests.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date
setupSteps.body: Set the required `start_date` field to the earliest date from which to sync. The connector pulls records whose `updated_at` is greater than or equal to this value.

setupSteps.title: Tune batch size (optional)
setupSteps.body: Optionally set the `batch_size` field, which is the number of days batched into a single request. Larger batch sizes reduce the number of API requests but increase response and processing time. Keep in mind Dixa rate limits each token to 10 requests per second and 864,000 requests per day.

sources.title: Tutorial: How to get access to the Dixa API (create an API token)
Dixa API Standards & Rules (authentication and rate limiting)
